comment_text.sn23.3:1.3: The bhavanetti is “that which leads to continued existence”. Early Sanskrit netrī always retains the expected grammatical sense, a noun from the root “to lead”, i.e. a “leader” (Rig Veda 1.113.4, 4.56.2, 5.50.2, 7.76.6, 7.77.2). Compare the English concept of the psychopomp or ferryman who leads you to the afterlife. Here, the commentary explains, “It is a name for craving. For by it beings are bound in their heart and lead on to life after life, like an ox tethered by the throat.” This suggests the sense of “lead” as in “leash”.

root_text.sn23.3:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā rādho bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn23.3:1.4: Katamā nu kho, bhante, bhavanetti, katamo bhavanettinirodho”ti?
root_text.sn23.3:1.5: “Rūpe kho, rādha, yo chando yo rāgo yā nandī yā taṇhā ye upayupādānā cetaso adhiṭṭhānābhinivesānusayā—

translation_text.sn23.3:1.2: Seated to one side, Venerable Rādha said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn23.3:1.3: “Sir, they speak of this thing called ‘the cessation of the leash to existence’.
translation_text.sn23.3:1.4: What is the leash to existence? And what is the cessation of the leash to existence?”
translation_text.sn23.3:1.5: “Rādha, any desire, greed, relishing, and craving for form; and any attraction, grasping, mental fixation, insistence, and underlying tendencies—
translation_text.sn23.3:1.6: this is called the leash to existence.
translation_text.sn23.3:1.7: Their cessation is the cessation of the leash to existence.
translation_text.sn23.3:1.8: Any desire, greed, relishing, and craving for feeling …
translation_text.sn23.3:1.11: consciousness; and any attraction, grasping, mental fixation, insistence, and underlying tendencies—
translation_text.sn23.3:1.12: this is called the leash to existence.
translation_text.sn23.3:1.13: Their cessation is the cessation of the leash to existence.”

variant_text.sn23.3:1.3: bhavanettinirodho → bhavanetti (bj, sya-all, km); bhavanettī (pts1ed) | bhavanettinirodho’ti → bhavanettīti (bj, sya-all, km); bhavanettīnirodhoti (pts1ed)
